claim: The text-only section (7 folios, entropy 3.9016 bits â€” highest of all sections) represents unenciphered or minimally enciphered running text in a natural language, while all other sections apply an additional layer of encoding (transposition, null insertion, or homophone expansion) that suppresses entropy, meaning the text-only section should serve as the primary decipherment anchor.
evidence_for: Text-only section entropy (3.9016) is closest to natural language entropy for Latin (~4.0) and Italian (~3.95) of any section
A section without illustrations would have no need for label-encoding or diagram-specific cipher conventions
Current belief at 0.677 supports text-only section as closest to natural language
The entropy gap between text-only (3.9016) and lowest section zodiac (3.7149) is 0.187 bits â€” substantial enough to suggest a different encoding regime
evidence_against: Text-only section is only 7 folios (2,349 words) â€” too small for robust statistical conclusions
Higher entropy could reflect a different topic domain rather than less encipherment
If text-only used a different (less redundant) cipher, entropy would also be higher without implying less encipherment
test: Compute bigram and trigram conditional entropy for the text-only section and compare to all other sections. Apply a Markov model trained on medieval Latin to score the text-only section vs. herbal section: compute the log-likelihood per word of each section under the Latin Markov model after optimal glyph-to-letter frequency mapping. Also test whether text-only word length distribution matches Latin word length distribution more closely than other sections (KL divergence).
test_metric: Log-likelihood per word under frequency-mapped Latin Markov model for text-only vs. herbal; KL divergence between text-only word length distribution and medieval Latin word length distribution
test_threshold: Confirm if text-only log-likelihood is >0.15 nats/word higher than herbal under the Latin model AND KL divergence to Latin word lengths is <0.08 bits; deny if text-only shows no statistical advantage over herbal under the Latin model

reasoning: If text-only section is demonstrably closer to natural language than other sections, it becomes the highest-value decipherment entry point. Confirming this would concentrate computational effort on the 7 most tractable folios and potentially yield a partial key applicable to the rest of the manuscript.
